raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/2025, I viewed an item ( XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX  ) on XXXX. I thought it was too ridiculous a price, so I wrote to the individual who posted the item. No response as I had asked about XXXX. I did not ever push Purchase for this item. Before I realized what had happened, the lawn tractor charge of {$580.00} appeared on my Citicard credit card. I contacted XXXX to tell them I had not purchased the mower and that they did not have my current credit card number in my profile ( it had been changed in late XXXX ), but they said the purchase had gone through and they could not cancel it. I then contacted Citicard to let them know about the transaction and wanted to cancel it. They would not remove the charge from my account. When I argued that XXXX did not have my new credit card number, the lady I spoke with at Citicard claimed that they did indeed have it and the purchase was put on my new account number. When I then told her to cancel the charge, she would not, saying that XXXX would need to remove the charge and until that happened, the incident would be put on dispute. 
I contacted XXXX to cancel the order ( that I had not made ), they would not cancel it, saying that because it was under dispute with Citicard, they would not be communicating with me going forward. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a message that included a tracking number for the shipment that was being delivered to my local post office and be waiting in a parcel locker. When I took the tracking number to the post office, the post mistress looked up the information and found that the item with that particular tracking number was not addressed to me, but to another individual who happens to receive his mail at the same post office. She was able to provide me with a photo of the parcel ( that weighed a mere XXXX ounces - obviously NOT a lawn tractor ) and wrote and signed a statement stating such. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, I sent to Citicard in South Dakota a priority mail envelope containing several items of proof that I had not received a lawn tractor from XXXX. I sent a letter detailing what had transpired in the communication between me and XXXX, me and Citicard, and my interactions with XXXX. They received these items on XX/XX/XXXX. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, Citicard communicated with me via letter that they were denying the dispute due to the fact that they claimed I had not provided proof of cancellation, nor proof of return. They ended by saying to contact the vendor directly again to pursue the matter further.
